architecture.test_framework: bun test (Bun v1.x) with Effect's testEffect harness
architecture.base_layer: packages/opencode/test/lib/effect.ts — testEffect(Layer) returns { effect, live, instance }
architecture.schema_validation: packages/opencode/src/tool/rig-schema-validate.ts — custom tree-walk validator (not full JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 — see BKM-001)

underlying_knowledge.BKM-001: The existing rig_schema_validate.ts tool does custom tree-walk validation (type checks, required props, array items). It does NOT support Draft 2020-12 keywords: $ref, allOf/oneOf/anyOf, if/then/else, patternProperties, unevaluatedProperties, contains, minContains, dependentRequired, dependentSchemas, propertyNames. The review_manifest schema MUST stay within the subset the validator supports, OR tests must use a different validation library.
underlying_knowledge.BKM-002: Snapshot.Service.diffFull(from, to) is the canonical diff source. It returns FileDiff[] with file?, patch?, additions, deletions, status?. The comparison engine MUST consume this shape.
underlying_knowledge.BKM-003: Plan claim_atoms are string[] with no structured file references. Matching claim_atoms to diffs requires fuzzy heuristics: token overlap, file-path mention, boundary-string containment. The engine must report a confidence score per atom.
underlying_knowledge.BKM-004: BinderService.getBinders() returns [] — binder persistence is stubbed. The engine MUST NOT depend on binder data. Use plan artifacts and snapshot diffs directly.
underlying_knowledge.BKM-005: 5 incompatible validation report schemas exist with no common schema. A normalizer module is needed before the manifest can aggregate validation summaries.
underlying_knowledge.BKM-006: No Python runtime or pyproject.toml exists. The `scripts/rig_relay_validate_schemas.py` and `tests/coordination/test_schema_validation.py` mentioned in AGENTS.md do not exist. Schema Python validation is blocked until Python infra is set up.

deliverable_1_schema_validation.title: rig.relay.review_manifest.v1 JSON Schema — Validation Tests
deliverable_1_schema_validation.layers_needed: rig_schema_validate tool layer or a direct Schema.parseEffect usage

test_vectors.description: Valid minimal manifest — only required fields, no optional sections
test_vectors.rationale: Only required fields. No optional sections (validation_summary, red_team_summary, residual_risks). No review_weight_map entries but empty object is valid.

test_vectors.description: Full manifest with every optional section populated

residual_risks: Edge case: empty claim_atoms causes default pass

test_vectors.rationale: All optional sections present with valid sub-shapes. file-level entries in diff_summary, validation metrics, red_team data, weight entries, residual risks.

test_vectors.description: Missing required field: manifest_id
test_vectors.rationale: manifest_id is required per schema. Validator must reject with 'Missing required property 'manifest_id''

test_vectors.description: Missing required section: diff_summary
test_vectors.rationale: diff_summary is required. Schema marks it as required.

test_vectors.description: Invalid weight value (not in enum)
test_vectors.rationale: weight field must be one of: hidden, mechanical, low, medium, high, critical. 'super-critical' is not valid.

test_vectors.description: diff_summary.files entry missing required 'file' field
test_vectors.rationale: diff_summary.files[].file is required in the manifest (even though Snapshot.FileDiff has it optional)

test_vectors.description: Invalid date-time format in generated_at
test_vectors.rationale: generated_at uses format: date-time. 'not-a-date' must be rejected.

test_vectors.description: All required fields only — boundary-edge whitespace handling
test_vectors.rationale: Strings with whitespace are valid per schema (no pattern constraint on boundary). Demonstrates that the schema does NOT strip — that's validation in the engine layer.

test_vectors.description: Weight rationale array must be array of strings
test_vectors.rationale: rationale must be array of strings. A plain string violates the schema.

test_vectors.description: Empty claim_atoms in plan_summary is valid
test_vectors.rationale: Schema does not enforce minItems on claim_atoms. An empty array is structurally valid (engine layer should flag it).

deliverable_1_schema_validation.validation_approach: Use Effect Schema.Struct parsing as the primary validator (stronger than the rig_schema_validate tool). Use Schema.parseEffect(reviewManifestSchema) then assert Either.isRight/Left. For the actual schema JSON file, use bun test to load the schema file, validate JSON.parse succeeds, and check required fields present.

deliverable_2_comparison_engine.title: Comparison Engine — Plan Claim_atoms vs Diff Matching
deliverable_2_comparison_engine.needs_mock: Mock plan artifacts + mock FileDiff[] inputs. No Snapshot layer needed.

test_vectors.description: All claim_atoms matched by diff files — ideal case
test_vectors.rationale: Every claim atom references a file that appears in the diff. Perfect match.

test_vectors.description: Claim atom references file not in diff — out-of-scope claim

deviation_atoms.reason: No diff touches any file matching 'core.ts'

test_vectors.rationale: Claim references core.ts but diff only touches rate.ts. Engine must flag the gap.

test_vectors.description: Diff contains files outside plan boundary — unexpected changes
test_vectors.rationale: Main claim is fulfilled, but 2 files outside plan boundary were changed. Engine must report scope leaks.

test_vectors.description: No diffs at all — plan produced zero changes
test_vectors.rationale: Plan claims changes but zero diffs produced. Engine should report all atoms as untouched.

test_vectors.description: Claim atom matches via fuzzy name (partial file path overlap)
test_vectors.rationale: Claim 'rate limiter module' -> fuzzy matched to 'rate-limiter.ts'. Must report medium confidence since no explicit file path.

test_vectors.description: Multiple claim_atoms matched to a single diff file
test_vectors.rationale: Two atoms both reference migrate.ts. Single file can satisfy multiple claims.

test_vectors.description: Claim matches via boundary string containment
test_vectors.rationale: File path falls under the plan boundary prefix. Claim matched via heuristic token overlap.

test_vectors.description: Diff with null file path (legacy) — graceful degradation

warnings: Legacy FileDiff without file path — skipping file-level analysis

test_vectors.rationale: Snapshot.FileDiff allows optional file for legacy compat. Engine must skip without crashing.

test_vectors.description: Claim mentions a pattern not a file — boundary-only match
test_vectors.rationale: Atom mentions a pattern (console.log removal), not a file. Engine matches via boundary and reports low confidence but no deviation.

test_vectors.description: Binary file in diff — should be flagged as weight=hidden
test_vectors.rationale: Binary files have empty patch. Engine must not crash, flag as binary/hidden weight.

deliverable_2_comparison_engine.test_code_structure: Pure function testing: planVsDiff(plan, diffs) => DeviationReport. Each test case provides plan + diffs as inline objects. No effect context needed — can use plain describe/it from bun:test.

deliverable_3_weight_rubric.title: Weight Rubric — File/Path Pattern Classification

test_vectors.description: Node_modules or lockfile — classified as hidden
test_vectors.rationale: node_modules/ is generated/dependency directory. Reviewers never need to see it.

test_vectors.description: Bun lockfile — hidden
test_vectors.rationale: Lockfiles are generated. Not human-reviewable.

test_vectors.description: pnpm-lock.yaml — hidden
test_vectors.rationale: Standard lockfile pattern.

test_vectors.description: Pure rename (additions=deletions=old_content) — mechanical
test_vectors.rationale: Equal add/delete lines suggests rename or move. Classified as low-review-need.

test_vectors.description: Reformat/whitespace only — mechanical
test_vectors.rationale: Patch content is only whitespace/indentation changes. Mechanical classification.

test_vectors.description: Test file addition — medium
test_vectors.rationale: Test files (.test.ts, .spec.ts) deserve review for correctness but aren't production-critical.

test_vectors.description: Config file modification — high
test_vectors.rationale: Config files can silently change behavior. High review priority.

test_vectors.description: Security boundary file — high or critical
test_vectors.rationale: Auth/session files are security-sensitive. High weight.

test_vectors.description: IPC bridge file — critical
test_vectors.rationale: Core snapshot module is an IPC bridge between subsystems. Changes can cascade.

test_vectors.description: Generated file (.g.ts, .gen.ts) — hidden
test_vectors.rationale: Generated code (\.g\.ts, \.gen\.ts) is auto-produced. Not human-reviewable.

test_vectors.description: Migration file — medium
test_vectors.rationale: Migrations are important but generally follow a template. Medium classification.

test_vectors.description: Package.json dependency change — high
test_vectors.rationale: Dependency changes can introduce supply-chain risk. High weight when patch shows actual dep change (not just version bump metadata).

test_vectors.description: Documentation only — low
test_vectors.rationale: Documentation changes are low risk.

test_vectors.description: CSS/asset only — low
test_vectors.rationale: Styling changes are cosmetic and low risk.

test_vectors.description: New production file (no special pattern) — medium default
test_vectors.rationale: New production file with no special path patterns gets default medium weight.

test_vectors.description: Existing production file modified — high
test_vectors.rationale: Modification to existing production code is higher risk than new file.

test_vectors.description: Schema definition file — high
test_vectors.rationale: Schema files define data contracts. Breaking changes affect all consumers.

test_vectors.description: Diff hunk not covered by any claim_atom — high
test_vectors.rationale: When hunk content doesn't match the claim_atom description, weight escalates to flag unreviewed code.

test_vectors.description: Code changed after validation completed — critical
test_vectors.rationale: Any change after validation is the highest risk signal — validated code may no longer match what was tested.

test_vectors.description: Weight rationale must be an ordered list of applicable rules
test_vectors.rationale: Each applicable rule appends an entry to the rationale array. The final weight is max(rule weights). The rationale documents which rules fired.

deliverable_3_weight_rubric.test_code_structure: Pure function testing: classifyWeight({ file, additions, deletions, status, patch?, extra_context }) => { weight, rationale[] }. Priority-ordered rule list with first-match-wins semantics for path patterns, then additive rationale accumulation.

deliverable_4_schema_python_validation.title: Schema Python Validation — All 4 Schemas Pass rig_relay_validate_schemas.py
deliverable_4_schema_python_validation.note: This deliverable is BLOCKED until the Python infrastructure (pyproject.toml, Python runtime) is set up. AGENTS.md references scripts/rig_relay_validate_schemas.py but it does not exist.
deliverable_4_schema_python_validation.contingency: Until Python infra exists, validate schemas via the TypeScript rig_schema_validate tool or direct Schema.parseEffect in bun tests.

checks: All referenced $id values resolve to existing files
Reconciles v1 (22 fields) and v2 (14 fields) via oneOf
Aligns with 3 existing prepublication tools (admitted/blocked/inconclusive) field names

bun_validation_tests.description: Each schema file is valid JSON and loads without error
bun_validation_tests.test: For each file: Bun.file(path).json() resolves without throwing

bun_validation_tests.description: Each schema has required metadata fields
Each schema uses draft-2020-12 or draft-07
No schema contains Python syntax (regression test per AGENTS.md)

test_vectors.description: End-to-end: create tracked snapshot, modify files, diffFull, produce manifest
steps: Create snapshot A with initial files
Feed FileDiff[] + plan into comparison engine → deviation report
Feed deviation report into weight rubric → weighted files
test_vectors.expected: Manifest has: diff_summary.total_files=3, review_weight_map has entries for each file, plan_vs_diff_deviation calculated, manifest validates against schema

test_vectors.description: Integration with empty diff (no changes between snapshots)
steps: Feed empty diffs + plan into engine
Verify manifest has zero-file diff_summary, all claim_atoms untouched
test_vectors.expected: diff_summary.total_files=0, all claim_atoms reported as untouched

test_vectors.description: Integration with binary files in snapshot
steps: Write a binary file (.png), modify a text file, delete another text file
diffFull → FileDiff[] with binary file (empty patch)
Verify engine handles binary without crashing
Verify weight rubric classifies binary as hidden
test_vectors.expected: Binary file entry exists in diff_summary.files[], weight='hidden', no crash

test_vectors.description: Manifest fields are internally consistent
steps: Verify diff_summary.total_additions === sum of file additions
Verify diff_summary.total_deletions === sum of file deletions
Verify review_weight_map keys === subset of diff_summary.files[*].file

test_vectors.description: Error handling: Snapshot.Service unavailable degrades gracefully
steps: Verify structured error in output (not crash)
Verify error is reported in provenance with status information
test_vectors.expected: Generator returns a partial manifest with error details, does not throw

test_vectors.description: Boundary scoping: only files within plan boundary are weighted
steps: Create files both inside and outside plan boundary
Verify files_in_scope counts only src/engine/ files
test_vectors.expected: Only files matching boundary prefix are counted in files_in_scope

smoke_tests.name: Schema files are valid JSON
smoke_tests.command: for f in docs/schemas/rig.relay.review_manifest.v1.schema.json docs/schemas/rig.relay.builder_publication_record.v1.schema.json docs/schemas/rig.relay.verification_record.v1.schema.json docs/schemas/rig.relay.prepublication_review_cycle.v1.schema.json; do test -f "$f" && echo "EXISTS: $f" && python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('$f'))" && echo "VALID JSON: $f" || echo "FAIL: $f"; done
smoke_tests.expected_output: All 4 files exist and are valid JSON

smoke_tests.name: Review manifest schema can validate a minimal instance
smoke_tests.command: cd packages/opencode && bun -e "const s = JSON.parse(await Bun.file('../../docs/schemas/rig.relay.review_manifest.v1.schema.json').text()); console.log('Schema loaded:', s.title, '| required:', s.required?.length, 'fields')"
smoke_tests.expected_output: Schema loaded with title and required fields count

smoke_tests.name: Existing tests still pass after schema changes
smoke_tests.command: cd packages/opencode && bun test --timeout 30000 test/tool/rig-schema-validate.test.ts 2>/dev/null || echo 'No dedicated validator test yet; checking integration test exists'

smoke_tests.name: Weight rubric edge cases don't crash
smoke_tests.command: cd packages/opencode && bun test test/review-compression/weight-rubric.test.ts 2>/dev/null || echo 'Weight rubric tests not yet written'

smoke_tests.name: Comparison engine handles empty inputs
smoke_tests.command: cd packages/opencode && bun test test/review-compression/comparison-engine.test.ts 2>/dev/null || echo 'Comparison engine tests not yet written'

smoke_tests.name: Integration test with real git snapshot
smoke_tests.command: cd packages/opencode && bun test test/review-compression/manifest-integration.test.ts 2>/dev/null || echo 'Integration tests not yet written'

bisect_script.description: Validates each deliverable checkpoint is working. Run this to bisect which layer broke.

steps.checkpoint: Schema files exist and are valid JSON
steps.command: const files = ['docs/schemas/rig.relay.review_manifest.v1.schema.json', 'docs/schemas/rig.relay.builder_publication_record.v1.schema.json', 'docs/schemas/rig.relay.verification_record.v1.schema.json', 'docs/schemas/rig.relay.prepublication_review_cycle.v1.schema.json']; files.forEach(f => { const exists = await Bun.file(f).exists(); if (!exists) throw new Error('MISSING: ' + f); })

steps.checkpoint: Schema files are valid Draft 2020-12 JSON Schema
steps.test: Validate each schema has $schema == 'https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema', $id, title, type='object', properties, and required array

steps.checkpoint: Weight rubric classifies correctly (20 test cases)
steps.test: run weight-rubric.test.ts, expect all 20 cases to pass

steps.checkpoint: Comparison engine matches plan to diffs (10 test cases)
steps.test: run comparison-engine.test.ts, expect all 10 cases to pass

steps.checkpoint: Schema validation rejects invalid manifests (10 test cases)
steps.test: run schema-validate.test.ts, expect all 10 cases to pass
run manifest-integration.test.ts, expect all 6 cases to pass

existing_tests_to_run.reason: Exercises Snapshot.Service.diffFull() — the primary diff source for the review manifest. Must pass before integration tests.
Exercises the schema validation tool. If this exists, run it before schema tests to ensure the validator itself works.
Exercises plan creation and claim_atoms parsing. Comparison engine consumes plan shapes.
Integration test for snapshot + tool pipeline. Ensures Snapshot.Service works in full app context.
Regression test for FileDiff backward compat (missing patch field). Comparison engine must handle optional fields.
